original: King Cyrus "the Great" of Persia marches into Babylon, freeing Jewish captives and allowing them to return home
Conradin, the last legitimate male heir of the German Hohenstaufen dynasty of Kings and Holy Roman Emperors, is executed with Frederick I, Margrave of Baden by Charles I of Sicily
First trial for witchcraft in Paris
Giovanni A. Facchinetti is elected as Pope Innocent IX
Battle of Mbwila [Ambuila]: Portuguese forces defeat the Kingdom of Kongo and decapitate King António I of Kongo, also known as Nvita a Nkanga
England & Netherlands sign anti-French "Barrieretraktaat"
Severe earthquake in New England
Mount Hood in Oregon is named after the British naval officer Alexander Arthur Hood by Lt. William E. Broughton, who spotted the mountain near the mouth of the Willamette River
French troops occupy Venlo (modern south east Netherlands)
First Ohio River steamboat leaves Pittsburgh for New Orleans
First steam-powered warship, "Demologos," is launched in New York for the US Navy
First US college fraternity to have a fraternity house is founded

International Committee of Red Cross forms as result Geneva held conference (Nobel 1917, 1944, 1963)
Greek parliament accepts new Constitution
Mail packets "Rhone" & "Wye" capsize off St Thomas, Virgin Islands

Stanley Park dedicated in Vancouver, British Columbia
First election of Hawaiian Republic
In Amherst, Massachusetts, nurse Jane Toppan is arrested for murdering the Davis family of Boston with an overdose of morphine

Turkish warships storm Black Sea
German sailors refuse to obey orders to fight British naval forces and lead a revolt in the naval ports of Wilhelmshaven, beginning the German Revolution

Textile strike against lower wages begins in Enschede, Netherlands
Musical revue "Dixie to Broadway" opens at Broadhurst Theater
1st Eastern Canada night CF game: Oshawa vs Toronto Balmy Beach

French liner Normandie is launched
Golden Gate International Exposition closes (1st closure)

Seventh day of battle at El Alamein: Montgomery assault
Three allied officers escape out camp Stalag Luft 3
1st Polish Armoured Division liberates Breda, Netherlands
Cabadese 2nd Infantry division frees Goes South-Beveland
First ballpoint pen goes on sale, manufactured by Reynolds in the US (just beating biro)

Trial of art forger Han van Meegeren begins in Amsterdam

White Sox trade Joe Tipton to A's for Nellie Fox

Detroit Lions Wally Triplett achieves a kickoff return yardage of 294 yards against the LA Rams, the second highest in a single-game in NFL history
A Baltimore group purchases St Louis Browns
Sweden begins experimental television broadcasts from the Royal Institute of Technology
Belgium signs accord for 5 day work week (45 hours)
Emile Zatopek runs world record 15 mile (1:14:01) & 25,000m (1:16:36)
International Xzone of Tangier returns to Kingdom of Morocco
Suez Crisis erupts into war as Israel invades the Gaza Strip and the Sinai Peninsula, to be followed by a planned airborne assault by France and Britain
Dr F Mason Sones is first doctor to perform a coronary angiogram
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
10 nation soccer league to play all games on NY Randalls Is, announced
Chartered C46 carrying Cal State's football team crashes, kills 16

"Meet the Beatles" booklet is published
Bobby Simpson completes cricket century in each innings v Pakistan
Star of India & other jewels are stolen in NY
The United Republic of Tanganyika & Zanzibar renamed The United Republic of Tanzania
Town of Karmi'el founded in Galilee
The Who release single "My Generation" in UK
US performs underground nuclear test at Amchitka Island Aleutians

Expo 67 closes in Montreal, Canada
Galt MacDermot, Gerome Ragni and James Rado's hippie musical "Hair" opens off-Broadway at the Public Theatre, NYC, for a limited 6-week run

USSR performs nuclear test at Sary Shagan USSR

USSR performs underground nuclear test
The Electoral Reform Society calls for the introduction of Proportional Representation (PR) in elections in Northern Ireland
WYEA (now WLTZ) TV channel 38 in Columbus, GA (NBC) 1st broadcast
Don Cockroft of Cleveland Browns kicks 57-yard field goal
Law bans discrimination of sex or marital status in credit application

Texas' Russell Erideben kicks his 3rd 60 yard field goal of the season

550,000 Dutch demonstrate against cruise missiles
Notre Dame's Gil Fenerty rushes for 337 yards & 6 touchdowns

2,000 US anti-abortion protesters arrested for blocking clinics
China announces a herbal male contraceptive
First scheduled Soviet shuttle launch (postponed)
Jim Elliott (US) begins 24-hr paced outdoor race for 548.9 mi
NYC MTA opens 63rd street extension to subway

30 die in a (5.7) earthquake in Algeria
American commercial fishing vessel (F/V) 'Andrea Gail' and crew of 6 lost at sea near Sable Island in North Atlantic Ocean; story becomes basis for book and film "The Perfect Storm"
Buck Showalter replaces Stump Merrill as NY Yankee manager
"The Sign" single released in Europe by Ace of Base
Dow Jones Industrial Average reaches record 3687.86
Antonov AN-12A crashes short of runway at Ust-Ilimsk Airport (Irkutsk, Russia), possibly due to icing; all 23 on board killed

NY Lotto pays $60 million+
Price of the Sunday NY News increases from $1 to $1.25
Iraq's Revolution Command Council announces that it will no longer allow US citizens and US aircraft to serve with UN arms inspection teams

Hurricane Mitch, the second deadliest Atlantic hurricane in history, makes landfall in Honduras
South Africa's Truth and Reconciliation Commission presents its report, which condemns both sides for committing atrocities

Turkish Airlines flight 6491 with 39 passengers and crew is hijacked by a Kurdish militant, who orders the pilot to fly to Switzerland. The pilot lands in Ankara, tricking the hijacker into believing he is landing in Sofia to refuel.
Deadliest Indian Ocean tropical super cyclone hits Odisha, India, death toll 9,885, wind speed of 300 mph
ExxonMobil announces that a consortium it leads will spend $4 billion over 5 years to develop large offshore oil and natural gas fields in Russia's far eastern Sakhalin region

Ho Chi Minh City ITC Inferno, a fire destroys a luxurious department store with 1,500 people shopping. Over 60 people died and over 100 are missing. It is the deadliest disaster in Vietnam during peacetime.
In Rome, 25 European heads of state sign a Treaty establishing a Constitution for Europe
Islamic terrorists set off three bombs in Delhi, killing 62 people and injuring hundreds
Argentina elects its first female president, Cristina Fernandez de Kirchner
Delta Air Lines merges with Northwest Airlines, creating the world's largest airline and reducing the number of US legacy carriers to five
Record-breaking snowstorm in the northeastern United States leaves nearly 2 million residents without power for more than 36 hours
Hurricane Sandy makes landfall in New Jersey resulting in 110 deaths and $50 billion in damage and forces the New York stock exchange to close
Publishing companies Penguin and Random House merge to form the world's largest publisher
China announces the end of its one-child policy after 35 years
Paul Ryan (R-Wisconsin) is elected Speaker of the US House of Representatives, succeeding John Boehner (R-Ohio)
Lion Air flight JT 610 crashes into the sea just after takeoff near Jakarta, Indonesia, with the loss of all 189 on board
New research says cacao tree first used in Ecuador by Mayo Chinchipe culture, 5000 years ago, not in central America as previously thought
Storms in Italy kill at least 11 with 75% of Venice flooded and two tornadoes striking Terracina
World Scrabble Championship won for the fourth time by New Zealand Malaysian Nigel Richards with the word "groutier" (bad-tempered)
1.5 million people without power in California as utility company turns power off to try and avoid sparking more wildfires
Lebanon's Prime Minister Saad Hariri resigns after two weeks of nationwide protests and an economic crisis
Rising seas could endanger 150 million people worldwide, three times more than previously thought, according study by Climate Central published in "Nature Communications"
Former leader Jeremy Corbyn is suspended from the British Labour Party after saying a report into antisemitism in the party was "overstated"
India records more than 8 million COVID-19 cases, the second country after the US, with a death toll of 120,527
South Australian government axes the Adelaide 500 from 2021 Supercars Championship due to COVID-19 and falling revenue
Three people stabbed to death in church in Nice, France, in an terrorist attack, after similar attack and President Macron's defense of right to publish cartoons of Prophet Muhammad
Crowd surge at Halloween festivities in Seoul, South Korea, leaves 155 people dead
Misuzulu ka Zwelithini crowned Zulu King in Duban, South Africa in the first coronation since 1994 democratic elections
Twin car bombs by a jihadist group in Mogadishu, Somalia, kill 120 people
Turkey celebrates 100 years since the founding of its Republic
Extreme flash flooding in Spain's south-eastern Valencia region, caused by a year's worth of rain in eight hours, leads to at least 222 deaths
Large complex Maya city Valeriana rediscovered "by accident" using a Lidar laser survey, capable of supporting up to 50,000 people 750-850 AD, in Campeche state, Mexico
TsarIvan IVreturns to Moscow after conquering the Khanate of Kazan in the Siege of Kazan
English adventurer, writer and courtierWalter Raleighis beheaded for allegedly conspiring againstKing James Iof England

William Pennlands at what is now Chester, Pennsylvania
Opera "Don Giovanni," with music byWolfgang Amadeus Mozartand a libretto by Lorenzo Da Ponte, premieres at the National Theater of Bohemia in Prague

Getulio Vargas, President of Brazil, resigns

"Asterix" is first published in the French magazine "Pilote" by Rene Goscinny, illustrated byAlbert Uderzo

Edgar Kaufmann Jr. deeds his family property Fallingwater (1935), designed byFrank Lloyd Wright, to the Western Pennsylvania Conservancy
National Organization of Women (NOW) officially  established at an organizing conference in Washington D.C.;Betty Friedanis elected as President

Fantasy Records releases "Willy and the Poor Boys", the fourth studio album by the American rock bandCreedence ClearwaterRevival; their third album of the year is their most critically acclaimed, and includes the hits "Down on the Corner", and "Fortunate Son"
Tom Seavervoted NL Cy Young Award
Frank Zappa's musical mockumentary "200 Motels", starring The Mothers of Invention, Theodore Bikel, andRingo Starrpremieres in Beverly Hills, California

Billy Martinfired as Yankee manager (2nd time)

Pink Floydfinishes their final concert tour with a show at Earls Court in London, England
Space Shuttle Discovery blasts off on STS-95 with 77-year oldJohn Glennon board, making him the oldest person to go into space.

Arabic news network, Al Jazeera broadcasts an excerpt from a video ofOsama bin Ladenin which the terrorist leader first admits direct responsibility for the September 11, 2001 attacks and references the 2004 U.S. presidential election
Lula da Silvais elected President of Brazil defeating Geraldo Alckmin in the second round

Special Court of Oyer and Terminer, convened for Salem witch trials, dissolved by the Governor of Massachusetts Bay Colony
Stock market crash on Wall Street, known as "Black Tuesday," triggers the Great Depression
British Prime MinisterMargaret Thatcheropens the final stretch of London's Orbital Motorway, the M25. Then the world’s longest ring road at 117 miles (188.3 km).
